symptoms.symptom_name: Large-volume haematemesis (coffee-ground plus fresh red blood)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large-volume haematemesis is a defining feature of active upper-GI bleeding and a life-threat regardless of source.
Coffee-ground and fresh-red haematemesis with epigastric pain on an ulcer background points to a bleeding ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Faintness, profound weakness, pallor, clamminess, racing heart (patient-reported shock proxies)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxies for hypotension and tachycardia — sufficient in telehealth to infer circulatory compromise and escalate without a measured blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning epigastric pain radiating through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Boring epigastric pain to the back is typical of deep peptic ulceration and raises the possibility of penetration/perforation.
Severe transfixing epigastric pain warrants active consideration of impending or actual perforation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent refractory ulcers unresponsive to maximum-dose proton-pump inhibitor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of maximum acid suppression to heal recurrent ulcers is a hallmark of pathological gastrin-driven acid hypersecretion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Ordinary H. pylori / NSAID peptic ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ordinary ulcers usually heal on maximum PPI therapy; refractoriness argues against a routine cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic secretory watery diarrhoea, steatorrhoea and unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acid-driven inactivation of pancreatic enzymes causes secretory diarrhoea and steatorrhoea; combined with refractory ulcers this is the characteristic gastrinoma cluster.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension (BP 90/55), tachycardia (HR 122) and delayed capillary refill (4 s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician measurements of distributive/haemorrhagic shock — sealed; the AI Doctor infers shock from reported faintness and pallor, not from a measured blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly elevated fasting serum gastrin (with secretin stimulation testing)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fasting serum gastrin and secretin stimulation test
investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated gastrin with a paradoxical rise on secretin
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying gastrinoma is not confirmed; this is a hospital/outpatient investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated gastrin with a positive secretin test confirms gastrinoma — but only after emergency stabilisation, in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy showing an actively bleeding ulcer

investigation_details.expected_result: Actively bleeding or high-risk peptic ulcer amenable to endoscopic haemostasis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without endoscopic haemostasis the bleeding is uncontrolled; this is an in-hospital procedure the AI can only arrange via emergency escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy localises and treats the bleeding source and is the definitive acute investigation — hospital-based.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Gastrinoma clinical cluster (refractory ulcers + secretory diarrhoea + epigastric pain + anticipated hypergastrinaemia)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, treatment-refractory multi-ulcer disease, secretory diarrhoea/steatorrhoea, epigastric pain and markedly elevated gastrin form the characteristic pattern of a gastrin-secreting tumour.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Large-volume haematemesis with faintness, pallor and tachycardia defines a life-threatening bleed requiring immediate emergency escalation — independent of the underlying cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer and in-hospital resuscitation (not a telehealth investigation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only appropriate 'investigation' pathway via telehealth is immediate 000 escalation; all diagnostic testing (bloods, endoscopy, gastrin) happens in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting serum gastrin and secretin stimulation test
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the underlying gastrinoma once the patient is stabilised — a hospital/specialist test, deferred, never obtained in real time via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat oral proton-pump-inhibitor dosing at home as the management step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Increasing oral acid suppression does nothing for an active massive haemorrhage and dangerously delays emergency care — an error of commission to be scored against.
